1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Validate config.toml step-by-step. Each step must pass before the next runs."""
3	
4	import os
5	import sys
6	import subprocess
7	import tempfile
8	import tomllib
9	import re
10	import shutil
11	import socket
12	
13	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
14	OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))
15	CONFIG_PATH = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "config.toml")
16	LIBNN_ROOT = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "../../libnn"))
17	
18	GREEN = "\033[92m"
19	RED = "\033[91m"
20	YELLOW = "\033[93m"
21	RESET = "\033[0m"
22	BOLD = "\033[1m"
23	
24	def ok(msg):
25	    print(f"  {GREEN}[OK]{RESET} {msg}")
26	
27	def fail(msg):
28	    print(f"  {RED}[FAIL]{RESET} {msg}")
29	
30	def warn(msg):
31	    print(f"  {YELLOW}[WARN]{RESET} {msg}")
32	
33	def header(msg):
34	    print(f"\n{BOLD}=== {msg} ==={RESET}")
35	
36	def die(msg):
37	    fail(msg)
38	    sys.exit(1)
39	
40	
41	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Step 1: Load config.toml, verify all required keys exist
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	def step_load_config():
45	    header("Step 1: Load config.toml")
46	
47	    if not os.path.isfile(CONFIG_PATH):
48	        die(f"config.toml not found at {CONFIG_PATH}")
49	
50	    ok(f"Found {CONFIG_PATH}")
51	
52	    with open(CONFIG_PATH, "rb") as f:
53	        cfg = tomllib.load(f)
54	
55	    required = {
56	        "toolchain": ["bin_dir", "prefix"],
57	        "target": ["cpu", "fpga_config"],
58	        "qemu": ["binary", "vlen", "timeout"],
59	        "fpga": ["host", "port", "timeout"],
60	        "limits": ["max_iterations", "min_improvement_pct"],
61	    }
62	
63	    for section, keys in required.items():
64	        if section not in cfg:
65	            die(f"Missing section [{section}]")
66	        for key in keys:
67	            if key not in cfg[section]:
68	                die(f"Missing key [{section}].{key}")
69	            ok(f"[{section}].{key} = {cfg[section][key]}")
70	
71	    return cfg
72	
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Step 2: Toolchain - bin_dir exists, gcc exists, Andes flags work
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	def step_toolchain(cfg):
78	    header("Step 2: Toolchain")
79	
80	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
81	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
82	
83	    if not os.path.isdir(bin_dir):
84	        fail(f"bin_dir does not exist: {bin_dir}")
85	        # Suggest alternatives
86	        for candidate in [
87	            "/local/nick/build-ast540/bin",
88	            "/local/nick/build-system-3/toolchain/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin",
89	        ]:
90	            gcc = os.path.join(candidate, f"{prefix}gcc")
91	            if os.path.isfile(gcc):
92	                warn(f"Found alternative: {candidate}")
93	        die("Fix [toolchain].bin_dir in config.toml and rerun.")
94	
95	    ok(f"bin_dir exists: {bin_dir}")
96	
97	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
98	    if not os.path.isfile(gcc):
99	        die(f"gcc not found: {gcc}")
100	    ok(f"gcc: {gcc}")
101	
102	    # Version
103	    r = subprocess.run([gcc, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
104	    ok(f"version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
105	
106	    # Andes flags
107	    r = subprocess.run(
108	        [gcc, "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector", "-x", "c",
109	         "-c", "/dev/null", "-o", "/dev/null"],
110	        capture_output=True, text=True,
111	    )
112	    if r.returncode != 0:
113	        die(f"Andes flags not supported: {r.stderr.strip()}")
114	    ok("Andes flags OK (-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector)")
115	
116	    # GDB (warn only - needed for FPGA step)
117	    gdb = None
118	    for name in [f"{prefix}gdb-nopython", f"{prefix}gdb"]:
119	        path = os.path.join(bin_dir, name)
120	        if os.path.isfile(path):
121	            gdb = path
122	            break
123	    if gdb:
124	        ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
125	    else:
126	        warn("GDB not in toolchain (will try system GDB for FPGA step)")
127	
128	    return gcc
129	
130	
131	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# Step 3: Compile hello world
133	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
134	HELLO_SRC = '#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n'
135	
136	BUILD_DIR = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, "build")
137	
138	def step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir):
139	    header("Step 3: Compile hello world")
140	
141	    os.makedirs(BUILD_DIR, exist_ok=True)
142	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello.c")
143	    elf = os.path.join(BUILD_DIR, "hello.adx")
144	
145	    with open(src, "w") as f:
146	        f.write(HELLO_SRC)
147	
148	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src,
149	           "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector=zve64d", "-mvh", "-lm"]
150	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(cmd)}")
151	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
152	    if r.returncode != 0:
153	        die(f"Compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
154	
155	    ok("Compilation succeeded")
156	
157	    r = subprocess.run(["file", elf], capture_output=True, text=True)
158	    if "RISC-V" not in r.stdout:
159	        die(f"Not a RISC-V ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
160	    ok(f"ELF: {r.stdout.strip()}")
161	
162	    return elf
163	
164	
165	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
166	# Step 4: QEMU - binary exists, run hello world
167	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
168	def step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=False):
169	    header("Step 4: QEMU")
170	
171	    qemu_bin = cfg["qemu"]["binary"]
172	    vlen = cfg["qemu"]["vlen"]
173	
174	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_bin):
175	        die(f"QEMU binary not found: {qemu_bin}")
176	    ok(f"Binary: {qemu_bin}")
177	
178	    r = subprocess.run([qemu_bin, "--version"], capture_output=True, text=True)
179	    ok(f"Version: {r.stdout.splitlines()[0]}")
180	
181	    qemu_cfg = os.path.join(
182	        LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
183	    )
184	    if not os.path.isfile(qemu_cfg):
185	        die(f"QEMU config not found: {qemu_cfg}")
186	    ok(f"Config: {qemu_cfg}")
187	
188	    # QEMU semihosting never exits after main() returns - kill after
189	    # enough time for hello world to print.
190	    hello_timeout = 10
191	    qemu_cmd = [
192	        qemu_bin, "-readconfig", qemu_cfg,
193	        "-M", "andes_ae350",
194	        "-cpu", f"andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen={vlen}",
195	        "-nographic", "-semihosting", "-bios", elf,
196	    ]
197	    ok(f"cmd: {' '.join(qemu_cmd)}")
198	
199	    if use_strace:
200	        # LLM sandbox: semihosting output goes to fd 9, not stdout.
201	        # Use strace to capture write syscalls and parse output from there.
202	        strace_log = elf + ".strace"
203	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout),
204	                   "strace", "-f", "-e", "trace=write", "-o", strace_log] + qemu_cmd
205	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, strace, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
206	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
207	
208	        if "HELLO_OK" in (r.stdout or ""):
209	            ok("Output captured via stdout: HELLO_OK")
210	            return
211	
212	        if os.path.isfile(strace_log):
213	            with open(strace_log) as f:
214	                content = f.read()
215	            if "HELLO_OK" in content:
216	                ok("Output captured via strace (semihosting fd): HELLO_OK")
217	                return
218	
219	        die("HELLO_OK not found in QEMU output (checked stdout + strace)")
220	    else:
221	        # Semihosting writes directly to the terminal, not through stdout.
222	        # Let output flow to the user's terminal; verify QEMU didn't crash.
223	        run_cmd = ["timeout", str(hello_timeout)] + qemu_cmd
224	        ok(f"Running hello.adx (VLEN={vlen}, timeout={hello_timeout}s) ...")
225	        r = subprocess.run(run_cmd, timeout=hello_timeout + 10)
226	
227	        if r.returncode == 124:
228	            # timeout killed QEMU - expected, semihosting never exits
229	            ok("QEMU ran until timeout (check terminal for HELLO_OK)")
230	            return
231	        elif r.returncode == 0:
232	            ok("QEMU exited cleanly")
233	            return
234	        else:
235	            die(f"QEMU crashed (rc={r.returncode})")
236	
237	
238	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
239	# Step 5: FPGA .inc - parse hardware features
240	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
241	def step_fpga_config(cfg):
242	    header("Step 5: FPGA .inc config")
243	
244	    path = cfg["target"]["fpga_config"]
245	    if not os.path.isabs(path):
246	        path = os.path.join(OPT_DIR, path)
247	
248	    if not os.path.isfile(path):
249	        die(f"FPGA config not found: {path}")
250	    ok(f"File: {path}")
251	
252	    with open(path) as f:
253	        content = f.read()
254	
255	    int_keys = {
256	        "NDS_VLEN": "vlen",
257	        "NDS_DLEN": "dlen",
258	        "NDS_ELEN": "elen",
259	        "NDS_DCACHE_SIZE_KB": "dcache_kb",
260	        "NDS_DCACHE_WAY": "dcache_way",
261	        "NDS_ICACHE_SIZE_KB": "icache_kb",
262	        "NDS_CACHE_LINE_SIZE": "cache_line",
263	        "NDS_L2C_CACHE_SIZE_KB": "l2_kb",
264	        "NDS_L2C_WAY": "l2_way",
265	        "NDS_L2_DATA_WIDTH": "l2_data_width",
266	        "NDS_BIU_DATA_WIDTH": "biu_data_width",
267	    }
268	
269	    # Boolean defines use "yes"/"no" strings or _INT suffix with 0/1
270	    bool_keys = {
271	        "NDS_RVV_SUPPORT": "rvv",
272	        "NDS_VECTOR_VDOT_SUPPORT": "vdot",
273	        "NDS_INT4_VECTOR_LOAD_SUPPORT": "int4_vload",
274	        "NDS_BFLOAT16_SUPPORT": "bf16",
275	        "NDS_QMAC_SUPPORT": "qmac",
276	    }
277	
278	    features = {}
279	    for vkey, jkey in int_keys.items():
280	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+(\d+)', content)
281	        if m:
282	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1))
283	            ok(f"{vkey} = {m.group(1)}")
284	        else:
285	            warn(f"{vkey} not found")
286	
287	    for vkey, jkey in bool_keys.items():
288	        # Try _INT variant first (numeric), then "yes"/"no" string
289	        m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}_INT\s+(\d+)', content)
290	        if m:
291	            features[jkey] = int(m.group(1)) != 0
292	        else:
293	            m = re.search(rf'`define\s+{vkey}\s+"(yes|no)"', content)
294	            if m:
295	                features[jkey] = m.group(1) == "yes"
296	            else:
297	                features[jkey] = False
298	
299	        status = "enabled" if features[jkey] else "disabled"
300	        ok(f"{vkey} = {status}")
301	
302	    return features
303	
304	
305	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
306	# Step 6: FPGA board connectivity
307	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
308	def step_fpga_connectivity(cfg):
309	    header("Step 6: FPGA board connectivity")
310	
311	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
312	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
313	
314	    try:
315	        sock = socket.create_connection((host, port), timeout=5)
316	        sock.close()
317	        ok(f"TCP {host}:{port} open")
318	    except (socket.timeout, ConnectionRefusedError, OSError) as e:
319	        die(f"Cannot connect to {host}:{port}: {e}")
320	
321	
322	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
323	# Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA board
324	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
325	def step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir):
326	    header("Step 7: Run hello world on FPGA")
327	
328	    bin_dir = cfg["toolchain"]["bin_dir"]
329	    prefix = cfg["toolchain"]["prefix"]
330	    host = cfg["fpga"]["host"]
331	    port = cfg["fpga"]["port"]
332	
333	    gcc = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gcc")
334	    ld_script = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/ae350-xip_hvm.ld")
335	    sim_wrapper = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board")
336	
337	    if not os.path.isfile(sim_wrapper):
338	        die(f"sim-wrapper not found: {sim_wrapper}")
339	
340	    # Find a working GDB. Prefer toolchain gdb to match the toolchain.
341	    gdb = None
342	    for candidate in [
343	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython"),
344	        os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb"),
345	    ]:
346	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
347	            # Quick check: can it run at all?
348	            r = subprocess.run([candidate, "--version"],
349	                               capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
350	            if r.returncode == 0:
351	                gdb = candidate
352	                break
353	    if not gdb:
354	        die("No working riscv64-elf-gdb found (needed for FPGA board runs)")
355	    ok(f"GDB: {gdb}")
356	
357	    # Compile with FPGA linker script
358	    src = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.c")
359	    elf = os.path.join(tmpdir, "hello_fpga.adx")
360	    with open(src, "w") as f:
361	        f.write('#include <stdio.h>\nint main(void) { printf("HELLO_FPGA_OK\\n"); return 0; }\n')
362	
363	    cmd = [gcc, "-O2", "-o", elf, src,
364	           "-mtune=andes-45-series", "-mext-vector=zve64d", "-mvh"]
365	    if os.path.isfile(ld_script):
366	        cmd.append(f"-T{ld_script}")
367	    cmd.append("-lm")
368	
369	    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
370	    if r.returncode != 0:
371	        die(f"FPGA compilation failed:\n{r.stderr}")
372	    ok("Compiled for FPGA")
373	
374	    # Remove First_Reset_done so wrapper resets the board
375	    first_reset = os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done")
376	    if os.path.isfile(first_reset):
377	        os.remove(first_reset)
378	
379	    env = os.environ.copy()
380	    env["MYPC"] = host
381	    env["MYPORT"] = str(port)
382	    env["PATH"] = f"{bin_dir}:{env.get('PATH', '')}"
383	
384	    # The expect script hardcodes riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython.
385	    # If missing, create a symlink in tmpdir and prepend to PATH.
386	    gdb_nopy = os.path.join(bin_dir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
387	    if not os.path.isfile(gdb_nopy):
388	        link = os.path.join(tmpdir, f"{prefix}gdb-nopython")
389	        os.symlink(gdb, link)
390	        env["PATH"] = f"{tmpdir}:{env['PATH']}"
391	        ok(f"Symlinked {prefix}gdb-nopython -> {os.path.basename(gdb)}")
392	
393	    ok(f"cmd: {sim_wrapper} {elf}")
394	    ok(f"  -> expect {os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, 'project/ax45mpv/gdb-ice-riscv64.exp')} {elf} ...")
395	    ok(f"  -> gdb: riscv64-elf-gdb-nopython -e {elf} -return-child-result -q")
396	    ok(f"  -> target remote {host}:{port}")
397	    ok(f"Running on FPGA ({host}:{port}, timeout=120s) ...")
398	    try:
399	        r = subprocess.run(
400	            [sim_wrapper, elf],
401	            capture_output=True, text=True,
402	            timeout=120, env=env,
403	            cwd=os.path.join(LIBNN_ROOT, "project/ax45mpv"),
404	        )
405	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
406	        die("FPGA run timed out (120s)")
407	
408	    combined = (r.stdout or "") + (r.stderr or "")
409	    if "HELLO_FPGA_OK" in combined:
410	        ok("FPGA output: HELLO_FPGA_OK")
411	        return
412	
413	    if r.returncode == 0:
414	        ok(f"FPGA returned 0 (GDB output may not be captured)")
415	        warn(f"stdout tail: {(r.stdout or '')[-200:]}")
416	        return
417	
418	    die(f"FPGA run failed (rc={r.returncode})\n  stdout: {(r.stdout or '')[-300:]}\n  stderr: {(r.stderr or '')[-300:]}")
419	
420	
421	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
422	# Main - sequential, fail-fast
423	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
424	def main():
425	    import argparse
426	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Validate config.toml step-by-step")
427	    parser.add_argument("--strace", action="store_true",
428	                        help="Use strace to capture QEMU semihosting output (for LLM sandbox)")
429	    args = parser.parse_args()
430	
431	    print(f"{BOLD}config.toml validation - sequential fail-fast{RESET}")
432	    print(f"Config: {CONFIG_PATH}")
433	    print(f"libnn:  {LIBNN_ROOT}")
434	
435	    cfg = step_load_config()
436	    gcc = step_toolchain(cfg)
437	
438	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="validate_") as tmpdir:
439	        elf = step_compile(cfg, gcc, tmpdir)
440	        step_qemu(cfg, elf, use_strace=args.strace)
441	        step_fpga_config(cfg)
442	        step_fpga_connectivity(cfg)
443	        step_fpga_run(cfg, tmpdir)
444	
445	    header("All steps passed")
446	
447	
448	if __name__ == "__main__":
449	    main()

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	################################################################################
3	# Build and run a single libnn unit test on Andes QEMU (AX45MPV)
4	#
5	# Usage:
6	#   run_qemu_test.sh <test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]
7	#
8	# Examples:
9	#   run_qemu_test.sh conv_HWC_s8_s8_s8_asym_bias_any --strace --vlen 1024
10	#   run_qemu_test.sh relu_s8 --strace --vlen 512
11	#
12	# Notes:
13	#   - In Claude Code's sandbox, QEMU semihosting output goes to fd 9, not
14	#     stdout. The --strace flag uses strace to capture fd 9 writes and parse
15	#     "accuracy checking pass/fail".
16	#   - QEMU must be run from Examples/unit_func/ because test binaries open
17	#     golden data via relative paths (e.g., "bin/8b_in1.bin").
18	################################################################################
19	set -uo pipefail
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
22	LIB_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/.." && pwd)"
23	
24	# Defaults
25	USE_STRACE=0
26	VLEN=1024
27	TIMEOUT=120
28	TEST_NAME=""
29	
30	# Parse arguments
31	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
32	    case "$1" in
33	        --strace)  USE_STRACE=1; shift ;;
34	        --vlen)    VLEN="$2"; shift 2 ;;
35	        --timeout) TIMEOUT="$2"; shift 2 ;;
36	        -*)        echo "Unknown option: $1" >&2; exit 1 ;;
37	        *)         TEST_NAME="$1"; shift ;;
38	    esac
39	done
40	
41	if [ -z "${TEST_NAME}" ]; then
42	    echo "Usage: $0 <test_name> [--strace] [--vlen N] [--timeout N]" >&2
43	    exit 1
44	fi
45	
46	# Paths
47	TOOLCHAIN_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
48	QEMU_BIN="/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64"
49	QEMU_CFG="${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/qemu_cfg/zve64d/ADP-AE350-AX45MPV-1C_dw0_mw1.cfg"
50	UNIT_TEST_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
51	TEST_SRC="${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.c"
52	WORK_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/work"
53	
54	# Validate
55	for f in "${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}/riscv64-elf-gcc" "${QEMU_BIN}" "${QEMU_CFG}" "${TEST_SRC}"; do
56	    if [ ! -f "$f" ]; then
57	        echo "ERROR: Not found: $f" >&2
58	        exit 1
59	    fi
60	done
61	
62	export PATH="${TOOLCHAIN_BIN}:${PATH}"
63	mkdir -p "${WORK_DIR}"
64	
65	# Check libnn.a exists
66	LIBNN="${LIB_ROOT}/libnn.a"
67	if [ ! -f "${LIBNN}" ]; then
68	    echo "ERROR: libnn.a not found. Run build_libnn.sh first." >&2
69	    exit 1
70	fi
71	
72	# Compile
73	CC="riscv64-elf-gcc"
74	CFLAGS="-O3 -Wall -Werror -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wl,-gc-sections"
75	CFLAGS+=" -mvh -mzfh -DENA_FS_VS"
76	CFLAGS+=" -mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=zve64d -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000"
77	CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=0"
78	CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
79	INC="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I${UNIT_TEST_DIR}/include"
80	ADX="${WORK_DIR}/t_${TEST_NAME}.adx"
81	
82	echo "[compile] ${TEST_NAME}"
83	${CC} ${CFLAGS} ${INC} -o "${ADX}" "${TEST_SRC}" "${LIBNN}" -lm 2>&1
84	if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
85	    echo "RESULT: COMPILE_ERROR" >&2
86	    exit 1
87	fi
88	
89	# Run QEMU
90	# Must run from unit_func dir so semihosting relative file opens work
91	cd "${UNIT_TEST_DIR}"
92	
93	QEMU_CMD="${QEMU_BIN} -readconfig ${QEMU_CFG} -M andes_ae350 -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vext_spec=v1.0,vlen=${VLEN} -nographic -semihosting -bios ${ADX}"
94	
95	echo "[run] QEMU VLEN=${VLEN} (timeout=${TIMEOUT}s)"
96	
97	if [ "${USE_STRACE}" -eq 1 ]; then
98	    # Use strace to capture semihosting writes on fd 9
99	    STRACE_LOG="${WORK_DIR}/strace_${TEST_NAME}.log"
100	    timeout "${TIMEOUT}" strace -f -e trace=write -o "${STRACE_LOG}" ${QEMU_CMD} > /dev/null 2>&1
101	    QEMU_RC=$?
102	
103	    # Parse strace output for semihosting messages (fd 9 or fd 1)
104	    # The fd used by QEMU semihosting can vary across environments
105	    OUTPUT=$(grep -E 'write\((9|1),' "${STRACE_LOG}" 2>/dev/null | sed 's/.*write([0-9]*, "//; s/\\n".*//; s/".*//') || true
106	
107	    echo ""
108	    echo "--- semihosting output ---"
109	    echo "${OUTPUT}"
110	    echo "--- end ---"
111	    echo ""
112	
113	    # Check for pass/fail
114	    if echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qi "accuracy checking pass"; then
115	        PASS_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -ci "accuracy checking pass" || true)
116	        FAIL_COUNT=$(echo "${OUTPUT}" | grep -ci "accuracy checking fail\|accuracy checking FAIL" || true)
117	
118	        if [ "${FAIL_COUNT}" -gt 0 ]; then
119	            echo "RESULT: FAIL (${FAIL_COUNT} failures detected)"
120	            exit 1
121	        else
122	            echo "RESULT: PASS (${PASS_COUNT} sub-tests passed)"
123	            exit 0
124	        fi
125	    elif [ "${QEMU_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
126	        echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
127	        exit 1
128	    else
129	        echo "RESULT: UNKNOWN (no pass/fail detected, QEMU exit=${QEMU_RC})"
130	        # Dump last 20 lines of strace for debugging
131	        echo "--- strace tail ---"
132	        tail -20 "${STRACE_LOG}" 2>/dev/null || true
133	        echo "---"
134	        exit 1
135	    fi
136	else
137	    # Direct run (output may not appear due to fd 9 issue)
138	    timeout "${TIMEOUT}" ${QEMU_CMD} 2>&1
139	    QEMU_RC=$?
140	    if [ "${QEMU_RC}" -eq 124 ]; then
141	        echo "RESULT: TIMEOUT (${TIMEOUT}s)"
142	        exit 1
143	    fi
144	    exit ${QEMU_RC}
145	fi

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	################################################################################
3	# Build libnn for AX45MPV with RVV (VLEN=1024)
4	# Output: libnn.a and libnn.objdump at the repo root
5	################################################################################

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	###########################################################################
3	# This script is used to collect the libnn functions' performance with the
4	# built-in libraries.
5	#
6	# Input:
7	#  $1: CPU name
8	#      supported CPU list:
9	#      (1) CPU w/o VPU:      n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45,
10	#                            a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
11	#      (2) 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64,
12	#                            nx27v_purec
13	#      (3) 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64,
14	#                            ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg,
15	#                            ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
16	#      (4) 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv,
17	#                            ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64,
18	#                            ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg,
19	#                            ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
20	#      (5) 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg,
21	#                            ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg,
22	#                            ax66_purec
23	#  $2: compiler name
24	#      Now only the elf toolchains are supported and the supported compilers
25	#      are:
26	#        - riscv32-elf-gcc
27	#        - riscv32-elf-clang
28	#        - riscv64-elf-gcc
29	#        - riscv64-elf-clang
30	#  $3: the target to run the executables
31	#      Now the target could only be BOARD.
32	#  $4: (optional) single function name to test
33	#      When specified, only this function is compiled and run.
34	#
35	# Example:
36	#  ex-1: measure the performance on FPGA with D25 bitmap and clang compiler
37	#  ./test_perf.sh "d25" "riscv32-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
38	#
39	#  ex-2: measure the performance on FPGA with AX25 bitmap and clang compiler
40	#  ./test_perf.sh "ax25" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
41	#
42	#  ex-3: measure the performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap and gcc compiler
43	#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v" "riscv64-elf-gcc" "BOARD"
44	#
45	#  ex-4: measure the pure C algorithm performance on FPGA with NX27V bitmap
46	#  and clang compiler
47	#  ./test_perf.sh "nx27v_purec" "riscv64-elf-clang" "BOARD"
48	#
49	#  ex-5: measure a single function with defaults (ax45mpv)
50	#  ./test_perf.sh "" "" "" avepool_HWC_s8
51	###########################################################################
52	
53	##### Environment setup for Andes toolchain and FPGA board #####
54	AST530_BIN="/local/nick/SW_Release_cp/ast530/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d/bin"
55	if [[ -d "${AST530_BIN}" ]] && [[ ":${PATH}:" != *":${AST530_BIN}:"* ]]; then
56	    export PATH="${AST530_BIN}:${PATH}"
57	fi
58	export MYPC="${MYPC:-sw-boards.andestech.com}"
59	export MYPORT="${MYPORT:-1115}"
60	
61	CPU_NAME=${1:-ax45mpv}
62	CPU_NAME=${CPU_NAME,,}   ## always convert to lower case
63	COMPILER_NAME="${2:-riscv64-elf-gcc}"
64	
65	if [ -z ${3} ]; then
66	    TARGET="BOARD"
67	else
68	    TARGET="${3}"
69	fi
70	
71	SINGLE_FUNC="$4"
72	
73	function log () {
74	    echo "$*" | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
75	}
76	
77	##### misc settings #####
78	LIB_NAME="libnn"
79	LIB_ROOT=$(readlink -f `dirname ${0}`)
80	rm -f "${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/First_Reset_done"
81	TEST_LOG="test_perf.log"
82	ERROR_LOG="diff_log"
83	
84	# set the proper cflag accrording to the CPU_NAME
85	if [[ ${CPU_NAME} == n225 ]]; then
86	    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-zc"
87	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == d23 ]]; then
88	    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp -mext-zc"
89	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?25$ ]]; then
90	    CPU_FLAGS=""
91	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)25$ ]] ||
92	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a27$ ]]; then
93	    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-dsp"
94	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^n(x)?45$ ]]; then
95	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series"
96	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|d|ax)45$ ]]; then
97	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp"
98	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mp$ ]]; then
99	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-dsp -mext-zc"
100	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
101	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
102	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
103	    CPU_FLAGS="-mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
104	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
105	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
106	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg)?$ ]]; then
107	    ${COMPILER_NAME} -E -dM - < /dev/null | grep -qw "__riscv_float_abi_soft"
108	    is_soft_ft_abi=$?
109	    if [[ ${is_soft_ft_abi} -eq 0 ]]; then
110	        VEC_CFG="zve32x"
111	    else
112	        VEC_CFG="zve32f"
113	    fi
114	
115	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector=${VEC_CFG} -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
116	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg?)?$ ]]; then
117	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-45-series -mext-vector -mext-zc -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
118	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg)?$ ]]; then
119	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -mext-vector -DNDS_VEC_RVV_VERSION=1000 -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
120	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv)_purec$ ]]; then
121	    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
122	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(a|ax)46mpv_purec$ ]]; then
123	    CPU_FLAGS="-DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP -mext-zc"
124	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax66_purec ]]; then
125	    CPU_FLAGS="-mtune=andes-60-series -DENA_RUN_TWICE -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
126	else
127	    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
128	    echo "Supported CPUs are listed below:
129	- CPU w/o VPU: n225, d23, n25, a25, d25, nx25, ax25, a27, n45, a45, d45, nx45, ax45, a46mp, ax46mp, ax65
130	- 27-series w/ VPU: nx27v, nx27v_elen64, nx27v_elen64_felen64, nx27v_purec
131	- 45-series w/ VPU: ax45mpv, ax45mpv_elen64, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64, ax45mpv_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_seg, ax45mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax45mpv_purec
132	- 46-series w/ VPU: a46mpv, a46mpv_seg, a46mpv_purec, ax46mpv, ax46mpv_elen64, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64, ax46mpv_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_seg, ax46mpv_elen64_felen64_seg, ax46mpv_purec
133	- 60-series w/ VPU: ax66, ax66_elen64, ax66_elen64_felen64, ax66_seg, ax66_elen64_seg, ax66_elen64_felen64_seg, ax66_purec"
134	
135	    touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
136	    exit 1
137	fi
138	
139	# check whether the compiler name is valid
140	if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|[nad]25|a27|[nad]45)$ ]] ||
141	   [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mp(v(_purec|_seg)?|)$ ]]; then
142	    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv32-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
143	        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
144	                "The available compilers are:\n" \
145	                "    riscv32-elf-gcc\n" \
146	                "    riscv32-elf-clang"
147	        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
148	        exit 1
149	    fi
150	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^[na]x(25|45)$ ]] ||
151	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_elen64(_felen64)?|_purec)?$ ]] ||
152	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(45|46)mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]] ||
153	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]] ||
154	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax(65|66)$ ]]; then
155	    if [[ ! ${COMPILER_NAME} =~ ^riscv64-elf-(gcc|clang)$ ]]; then
156	        echo -e "Error! Pleas set the compiler to an appropriate one.\n" \
157	                "The available compilers are:\n" \
158	                "    riscv64-elf-gcc\n" \
159	                "    riscv64-elf-clang"
160	        touch "../${ERROR_LOG}"
161	        exit 1
162	    fi
163	else
164	    # here will not be reached
165	    echo "Error! ${1} is not a supported CPU !"
166	fi
167	
168	# use the customized linker script for moving the data into local memory
169	if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n225|d23|n25|a25|d25|nx25|ax25|a27|n45|a45|d45|nx45|ax45|a46mp|ax46mp)$ ]]; then
170	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -DENA_LM"
171	fi
172	
173	# select the library to be linkd
174	if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)$ ]]; then
175	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v"
176	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64$ ]]; then
177	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64"
178	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(nx27v|ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64$ ]]; then
179	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64"
180	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|a46mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_seg$ ]]; then
181	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_seg"
182	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_seg$ ]]; then
183	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_seg"
184	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(ax45mpv|ax46mpv|ax66)_elen64_felen64_seg$ ]]; then
185	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn_v_elen64_felen64_seg"
186	else
187	    CPU_FLAGS+=" -lnn"
188	fi
189	
190	# select the running on board wrapper
191	if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax45mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
192	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax45mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
193	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^nx27v(_purec|_elen64|_elen64_felen64)?$ ]]; then
194	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/nx27v/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
195	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^(n|a)x(25|45)$ ]] ||
196	     [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax46mp ]]; then
197	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax25/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
198	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^a46mpv(_seg|_purec)?$  ]]; then
199	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/a46mpv/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
200	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax46mpv(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
201	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax46mpv/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
202	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} == ax65 ]]; then
203	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax65/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
204	elif [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ ^ax66(_elen64(_felen64)?(_seg)?|_seg|_purec)?$ ]]; then
205	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/ax66/riscv64-sim-wrapper-on-board)
206	else
207	   SIM_WRAPPER=$(readlink -f ${LIB_ROOT}/project/d25/riscv32-sim-wrapper-on-board)
208	fi
209	
210	# -----
211	# Automatically enable the zfh extension if the toolcahin supports it.
212	# Note-1. We should check whether the zfh extension could be enabled with
213	# all compilation flags. The zfh extension may be supported by the
214	# toolchain; however, it's disabled by some compilation flags.
215	# Note-2. Now the zfh extension is supported only on NX27V and 45-series
216	# cores.
217	# -----
218	# check whether the zfh extension could be enabled
219	${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null &> /dev/null
220	if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
221	    # check whether the toolchain is [single|double]-abi
222	    # (for the spcial case of v5 toolchain + clang)
223	    ${COMPILER_NAME} -mzfh -E -dM ${CPU_FLAGS} - < /dev/null | grep '__riscv_float_abi_single\|__riscv_float_abi_double' &> /dev/null
224	    if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
225	        # check whether the core is NX27V/45-series/46-series/60-series
226	        if [[ ${CPU_NAME} =~ (nx27v|45|46|65|66) ]]; then
227	            CPU_FLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS} -mzfh"
228	        fi
229	    fi
230	fi
231	
232	##### Link against source-built libnn #####
233	# Use the library at the repo root (built via ./build.sh AX45MPV) instead of
234	# the toolchain's built-in libnn_v.a.
235	BUILD_LIB_DIR="${LIB_ROOT}"
236	if [[ ! -f "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" ]]; then
237	    echo "Error: libnn.a not found at ${BUILD_LIB_DIR}. Run build.sh first."
238	    exit 1
239	fi
240	# The linker flag -lnn_v expects libnn_v.a. build.sh produces libnn.a, so
241	# create a symlink with the expected name.
242	ln -sf "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn.a" "${BUILD_LIB_DIR}/libnn_v.a"
243	# Prepend -L so the linker finds our library before the toolchain's built-in.
244	CPU_FLAGS="-L${BUILD_LIB_DIR} ${CPU_FLAGS}"
245	
246	##### main ######
247	rm -f ${TEST_LOG}
248	log "***************************************************************************************"
249	log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing begin ...
250	log "Input 1(CPU): ${CPU_NAME}"
251	log "Input 2(COMPILER): ${COMPILER_NAME}"
252	log "Input 3(TEST_TARGET): ${TARGET}"
253	log "BOARD: ${MYPC}:${MYPORT} (VLEN=1024)"
254	log "SIM_WRAPPER: ${SIM_WRAPPER}"
255	log "EXTRA_CFLAGS: ${CPU_FLAGS}"
256	if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
257	    log "SINGLE_FUNC: ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
258	fi
259	log "***************************************************************************************"
260	
261	EXTRA_CFLAGS="${CPU_FLAGS}"
262	
263	if [[ -n "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
264	    #####################################################################
265	    # Single-function mode: compile and run one test directly
266	    #####################################################################
267	    UNIT_TEST_PATH="${LIB_ROOT}/Examples/unit_func"
268	    SRC_FILE="${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.c"
269	    if [[ ! -f "${SRC_FILE}" ]]; then
270	        echo "Error: test source not found: ${SRC_FILE}"
271	        exit 1
272	    fi
273	
274	    # Build flags matching what test.sh uses for BOARD + v-ext perf tests
275	    APP_CFLAGS="-O3 -ffunction-sections -fdata-sections -Wall -Werror"
276	    APP_CFLAGS+=" -Wl,-gc-sections -DPF_COUNTER -mvh -DENA_FS_VS"
277	    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_FAST_ALGO -DENA_CACHEABLE_ENV_SETUP"
278	    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_DUMP_GOLDEN=0 -DENA_MEASURE_PERF=1"
279	    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
280	    APP_CFLAGS+=" -DGETENV_TESTDIR=${UNIT_TEST_PATH}"
281	    INC_DIR="-I${LIB_ROOT}/Include -I$(${COMPILER_NAME} -print-sysroot)/../../riscv64-elf/include -I${UNIT_TEST_PATH}/include"
282	    ADX_FILE="t_${SINGLE_FUNC}.adx"
283	
284	    build_cmd="${COMPILER_NAME} ${APP_CFLAGS} ${INC_DIR} -o ${ADX_FILE} ${SRC_FILE} ${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -lm"
285	    log "Build: ${build_cmd}"
286	    ${build_cmd} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
287	    if [[ ${PIPESTATUS[0]} -ne 0 ]]; then
288	        echo "Error: compilation failed for ${SINGLE_FUNC}"
289	        exit 1
290	    fi
291	
292	    log "Running ${SINGLE_FUNC} on board..."
293	    log "Start at $(date)"
294	    ${SIM_WRAPPER} ${ADX_FILE} 2>&1 | tee -a ${TEST_LOG}
295	    ret=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
296	    log "End at $(date)"
297	
298	    # Parse inst/cycle from output
299	    if [[ ${ret} -eq 0 ]]; then
300	        inst=$(grep -w 'inst' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
301	        cycle=$(grep -w 'cycle' ${TEST_LOG} | awk '{printf "%ld", $5}')
302	        log ""
303	        log "Result: ${SINGLE_FUNC}  inst=${inst}  cycle=${cycle}"
304	    else
305	        log "Error: board execution failed (ret=${ret})"
306	    fi
307	else
308	    #####################################################################
309	    # Full test suite mode
310	    #####################################################################
311	    ${LIB_ROOT}/test.sh "" "${COMPILER_NAME}" "${SIM_WRAPPER}" "${TARGET}" "BS3" "${EXTRA_CFLAGS} -DENA_TEST_PERF -DENA_DUMP_PARAMETERS"
312	fi
313	
314	log "***************************************************************************************"
315	log ${LIB_NAME} performance testing end.
316	log "***************************************************************************************"
317	
318	# move the log into test_sh_<TIMESTAMP> folder (full mode only)
319	if [[ -z "${SINGLE_FUNC}" ]]; then
320	    out_folder_list=($(find . -name "test_sh*" -type d | sort))
321	    mv "test_perf.log" -t "${out_folder_list[${#arr[@]}-1]}"
322	fi

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Resolve a short pattern to a full libnn perf test function name.
3	
4	Usage:
5	    python3 resolve_function.py <pattern>
6	
7	Pattern is space- or underscore-separated tokens matched as substrings
8	in order. E.g. "conv_dw u8" matches "conv_dw_HWC_u8_u8_u8_asym_bias_any".
9	
10	Exit codes:
11	    0 - exactly one match (printed to stdout)
12	    1 - zero matches
13	    2 - multiple matches (printed as numbered list to stderr, prompts user)
14	"""

16	import os
17	import re
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	SCRIPT_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
22	OPT_DIR = os.path.normpath(os.path.join(SCRIPT_DIR, ".."))

27	def find_matches(pattern: str, perf_dir: str | Path = PERF_DIR) -> list[str]:
28	    """Return function names matching pattern.
29	
30	    Tokens in pattern (split on whitespace) become .*-joined regex,
31	    matched case-insensitively against function names extracted from
32	    t_pf_<name>.c filenames.
33	    """
34	    perf_dir = Path(perf_dir)
35	    names = sorted(
36	        f.stem.removeprefix("t_pf_")
37	        for f in perf_dir.glob("t_pf_*.c")
38	    )
39	
40	    tokens = pattern.strip().split()
41	    regex = re.compile(".*".join(re.escape(t) for t in tokens), re.IGNORECASE)
42	
43	    return [n for n in names if regex.search(n)]
44	
45	
46	def main():

48	        print("Usage: resolve_function.py <pattern>", file=sys.stderr)
49	        sys.exit(1)

63	            print(f"  {i}. {name}", file=sys.stderr)

**Purpose:** Collect libnn performance metrics on FPGA board. Supports full test suite or single-function mode.

The baseline phase validates everything (config, toolchain, QEMU, FPGA), then measures unmodified library on FPGA via a simulation wrapper connected to real board at `MYPC:MYPORT`, parsing cycles/instructions from test output or performance.csv.
